ab: duru ma kɨkɨ kububu ru swa?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: nakuni, sakura kɨkɨ na madelame.
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: duru ma kɨkɨ kububu ru swa?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: éni, ikadro ku alriguali.
en: B:Don't, I don't know much about it.

ab: vévanenga ka iké swa samoru? pakdredreme kuvania kububuru ma kɨkɨ swa ipulri ngo?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I'd like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: margi, nukéma kɨkɨ dane drodro swa.
en: B: Okay, I'll wait for you at home.

ab: iké swa na samoru kusianga? kukbuburu nia ma kɨkɨ pakdredreme ku yienela na Drukaizuku tibuburu.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於茂林魯凱的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I would like to ask you about the legendary story of Maureen Rukai.

ab: kusianga ka nuua ma kɨkɨ rgerge, nukiakɨ dane.
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I'm going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won't be home.

ab: ubicilringi kɨkɨ na bka, musu ka sakura urgu ku tumane ni sarkiké ni, na Drukaizuku ma kukbuburu nia ma kɨkɨ swa pakdredreme.
ch: A:我聽說您對阿美族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you are very familiar with the traditional customs of the Amis, so I would like to ask you a few questions. *Please change [Amis] to your ethnic group.

ab: ikadro ku alriguali, makasi iku alriguali ka nubiri makswa.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don't know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: kukbuburu swa duru makɨkɨ lruing mia? inatuménia ka udaduru saulute.
ch: A:請問我可以錄影嗎?這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: margi, ipiringo.
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: kukbuburu swa pidra cvélé su cacvélé?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: mvangte kvaru nga kɨkɨ cvélé.
en: B: I'm 98 years old.

ab: thɨbarela musu.
en: A: You're so young.

ab: tédrala! marudrangeng kɨkɨ.
en: B: No, I'm not! I'm old.

ab: kukbuburula swa ikadra salekanesu mukuné?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: saleké kɨkɨ Punugu mukuné.
en: B: I'm from [Punugu].

ab: kukbuburu la swa iku asirasu ka Drukaizuku?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是魯凱族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also [a Rukai]?

ab: éni, Bunungzuku kini.
en: B: No, she's a [Bunun].

ab: aciku alrigua su musobaka na bka?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: urgu kɨkɨ Teldrekane si SoSongvane.
ch: B: 我會說阿美語和卑南語。
en: B: I speak [Aramaic] and [Puyuma].

ab: sakura kɨkɨ na mabebere duru kububuru swa, alamusobaka, tumani na ulupu ku kilrisiani ni?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It's a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: margi. iku athabrelila ka sakurakɨ sitalese mua rgerge ulupu.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: takedrengésu sesaku sububuru su sakura na margi.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: spasobkélinga matakadrengé kini, amani ki lomumu ku spasobké ni yienɨkini.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: sesa ku kibuburualeli tathémangakené, takedre ngé su tumasu nia masiarara.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That's all for today's interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: adraikénu, tapabkala.
en: B: You're welcome, let's talk again.

ab: nugia nga kɨkɨ, si skakulanega kedrama nladronga na sitibuburu.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: I'm going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: margi! relenga dodavece.
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
